source: External scanner issue names provided manually

rules.rationale: Maps cleanly to a stable lint threshold.

externalRuleNames: Enforce Medium Number of Lines of Code (NLOC) Limit
rules.rationale: Use lint-native function-size limits instead of a custom scanner.

externalRuleNames: Enforce Medium File Length Limit Based on Number of Lines of Code
rules.rationale: Use a standard file-length lint rule.
Maps directly to lint-native parameter count enforcement.

externalRuleNames: Disallow Trailing Commas in Array and Object Literals
Avoid Trailing Comma in Object or Array Literals
rules.rationale: Both names normalize to the same trailing-comma policy.
TypeScript module-resolution parity is implemented locally because eslint-plugin-import is not wired in this repo.
Prefer a targeted lint rule over a custom scanner.
Lint-native syntax restriction.
Lint-native async control-flow rule.
Implemented locally because the current ESLint flat-config stack does not expose the core rule cleanly.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Using Non-Literal User Input for File System Paths
Detect Non-Literal Filename in fs Calls
Avoid Path Traversal via path.join or path.resolve with User Input
Avoid Using Untrusted Input in readFile and readFileSync
rules.rationale: These are path-taint and file-system sink checks, not good ESLint-only rules.
Credential-pattern detection belongs in a scanner, not core ESLint.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Use of Cryptographically Weak Random Number Generators
rules.rationale: Requires API-usage scanning with security context.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Using Non-Literal Values in RegExp Constructor
Disallow Unused Capturing Group in RegExp
rules.rationale: These overlap around dynamic regex construction and regex safety.

externalRuleNames: Avoid NoSQL Injection via Untrusted Input in findOne()
rules.rationale: Query taint analysis is scanner territory.

externalRuleNames: Detect usage of dangerouslySetInnerHTML with non-constant definitions
rules.rationale: Already covered by the existing static security scanner.

externalRuleNames: Avoid assigning user-controlled data to innerHTML
Avoid User Controlled Data in HTML String Join
Disallow Mixed HTML and Unencoded Input to Prevent XSS
Avoid Using Insecure Document Methods with User-Controlled Data
rules.rationale: These are sink-oriented XSS checks not currently covered by ESLint.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) by Validating User-Controlled URLs
rules.rationale: Requires URL sink validation and taint awareness.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Timing Attack Vulnerabilities in String Comparisons
rules.rationale: Normalize repeated timing-attack findings into one scanner rule family.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Using Unsafe Dynamic Method Calls
rules.rationale: These are all dynamic object/property access safety checks.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Open Redirect Vulnerabilities in Express Redirects
rules.rationale: Redirect target validation is a dataflow check, even if Express-specific wording is legacy.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Multiple Headings with the Same Content
rules.rationale: A structural content check fits better in a dedicated scanner.

externalRuleNames: Disallow Access to Restricted Browser Globals During SSR
rules.rationale: Server/client environment misuse needs targeted app-specific scanning.

externalRuleNames: Disallow Sass Variables Without Interpolation in CSS Custom Identifiers
Enforce Numeric Values with Units Using Arithmetic in SCSS
Disallow Unknown Values for CSS Property Declarations
Disallow Global Function Names in Sass
Disallow Use of Obsolete HTML Attributes
rules.rationale: Frontend compatibility and style dialect checks belong in a dedicated scanner lane.

externalRuleNames: Enforce Pinning of Third-Party GitHub Actions to Full Commit SHA
rules.rationale: Workflow YAML policy checking is better handled outside ESLint.

externalRuleNames: Enforce JSDoc imports correspond to listed dependencies
rules.rationale: Dependency metadata checks belong in a documentation/package scanner.

externalRuleNames: Require Parameter Type Annotations in Flowtype
Disallow Missing React When Using JSX
rules.rationale: These are obsolete for a modern TypeScript + React + ESM codebase.

externalRuleNames: Detect Missing Template String Indicator in JavaScript
Disallow Reserved Words as Property Names
rules.rationale: These bans conflict with the current TypeScript/Next.js style and would create large amounts of low-signal noise.

externalRuleNames: Avoid Using JSON.stringify for Object Keys
Enforce Usage of Valid ECMAScript Intrinsic APIs
Forbid Using Named Export as Property of Default Export
rules.rationale: These are broad legacy or stylistic bans that do not align with the current codebase.

externalRuleNames: Forbid the use of null and undefined
rules.rationale: These are incompatible with the repo runtime model and existing script infrastructure.
This overlaps with a style preference from another ecosystem and is not appropriate as a repo-wide policy here.
